recipe.title: FRIED SQUASH OR COURGETTE FLOWERS

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 120g self-raising flour, plus extra for dusting
salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps.instruction: insects. To make the batter, place the flour in a bowl and slowly whisk in 200ml ice-cold water to form a thickish batter. Add the grated cheese, season with salt and pepper and give it a final whisk.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: vegetable or corn oil for deep-frying

steps.instruction: Heat an 8cm depth of oil in a deep-fat fryer or other suitable deep, heavy pan to 160-180°C, or until a little batter dropped into the oil turns golden brown after a minute or so.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 120g self-raising flour, plus extra for dusting
vegetable or corn oil for deep-frying

steps.instruction: Have a shallow bowl of flour ready. One at a time, dip the flowers into the flour, shaking off any excess, then dip into the batter to coat and drop into the hot oil. Deep-fry 3 or 4 flowers at a time, moving them around in the oil with a spoon, for a couple of minutes until they are crisp and light golden.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 8 large courgette, squash or pumpkin flowers,

steps.instruction: Remove the courgette flowers with a slotted spoon and put on a plate lined with kitchen paper to drain.

ingredient_lines.raw_text: salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps.instruction: Season lightly with salt and serve.
